---
title: "LRP-CA-006: Concurrent conditional associations — skills → expressive vocabulary (per wave)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP-CA-006 extends the **concurrent conditional-associations** family (#336, descriptive-association workstream #314) to **standardised expressive vocabulary** (EOWPVT, `E`) as the focal outcome. At each timepoint (t1–t4) it fits a between-child Beta-Binomial regression of expressive vocabulary's level on the standardised same-wave logits of the other core skills — word reading (`W`), letter sounds (`L`), blending (`B`), taught receptive (`TR`) and expressive (`TE`) vocabulary, and standardised receptive vocabulary (`R`) — plus age and a group nuisance term. The four waves are fitted separately and reported side by side.

The family's core skill set is {`W`, `L`, `B`, `TR`, `TE`, `R`, `E`}: each model conditions its focal outcome on the remaining six, so the models describe complementary full conditionals of the same measure set. The estimand is the mutually-adjusted association at each wave: *"at wave t, among children alike on age and the other skills, +n of a predictor skill is associated with +m more expressive-vocabulary items"*. `E` is a 170-item standardised transfer measure and does not have the late-wave ceiling compression flagged for the 24-item `TR` focal. All children are on the intervention from t2, so these associations describe the skill measures within a treated system.

::: {.callout-note title="Estimand: descriptive association (no causal warrant)"}
Every coefficient is an **adjusted association**. Conditioning on contemporaneous (post-treatment) skill levels is intentional and licensed *because nothing here is read causally*. Read the results with the Table-2-fallacy, measurement-error and collinearity/regularisation caveats set out in the Results section.
:::

## Design

- **Four separate cross-sectional fits** (one row per child per wave), reported side by side — not one stacked model with a child random intercept, which would tilt the coefficients toward a within-child quantity and change the estimand (issue #312 decision). The wave with the largest complete-outcome sample (ties → latest) is the operational diagnostic anchor for the standard trace and plots; this does not make it substantively primary or establish that it is best-powered. Every adjusted and bivariate fit has complete convergence metrics in the tables.
- **Predictors** enter as standardised same-wave logits with regularising `Normal(0, 0.3)` slopes (essential at n ≈ 53 with a collinear predictor cluster). Floored measures (`P`, `N`) are excluded as predictors.
- **Group** is included only as a non-interpretable nuisance term (absorbs arm composition at each wave); it is not reported as an association.
- **Missing predictor values** are mean-imputed (0 on the standardised scale). This can bias a conditional coefficient, with no guaranteed direction when missingness relates to the predictor, outcome or other skills; observed and imputed counts are reported. Rows missing the focal *outcome* are dropped.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_concurrent.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
